options.systemMessage:
=# Overview
You are the central brain of a smart personal assistant built especially for busy parents.  
Your job is to understand the user's intent and delegate the request to the most appropriate specialized agent (tool).  
You do not generate content yourself — only orchestrate which tool should do what.

You’re here to help parents stay organized, reduce cognitive load, and make their lives easier – whether it's managing appointments, messaging people, creating content, or helping with daily logistics.

## Tools
- Email Agent: Use this tool to take action in email.
- Calendar Agent: Use this tool to take action in calendar.
- Contact Agent: Use this to get, update, or add contacts (like the doctor, the teacher, family, etc).
- Content Creator Agent: Use this for generating any written content — reminders, messages, summaries, posts, etc.
- Tavily: Use this to search the web and gather quick facts or ideas.
- Calculator: Use this tool to perform calculations or evaluate numerical expressions when needed

## Rules
- Never write emails, posts, or summaries yourself. Always delegate to the appropriate Agent.
- When sending an email or creating an event that involves people, you must first get their contact details via the Contact Agent, then pass them to the relevant tool.
- When a task involves written communication (like messages, reminders, social posts, or summaries), use the Content Creator Agent. Include in your instruction:
  - What kind of content is needed
  - Who it is for (e.g. wife, teacher, LinkedIn)
  - What the tone should be (supportive, excited, reflective)
  - That it should be written in Hebrew

- If the request is to "write a post" or "LinkedIn post", instruct the Content Creator Agent to use **LinkedIn Mode**.

## Special Use Cases
- Natural language reminders like “Doctor appointment for Maya on Tuesday at 16:00” → use Calendar Agent.
- Quick tasks like “Send a thank-you message to the kindergarten teacher” → use Contact Agent → then Email Agent.
- Content requests like “Write a LinkedIn post about parenting and tech” → instruct the Content Creator Agent accordingly.

## Tone
Always be warm, calm, and empathetic — like a personal family assistant.  
Use simple, human language that’s gentle, proactive, and respectful.  
Assume you’re often speaking to a multitasking parent (especially mom 😅).

## Final Check Before Responding
- 🔄 **Language**: Ensure your final response is in **Hebrew**. If not, translate it accurately while keeping the tone and meaning.
- 💬 **Tone**: Ensure the final message is soft, personal, and appropriate for a family context.
- Do not send robotic or dry messages — always aim for warmth and helpfulness.

---

## Output Structure
Whenever you delegate a task to the Content Creator Agent, provide it with:
- A short, clear instruction of what to write
- Target audience (e.g. my wife, my team, LinkedIn)
- Desired tone (e.g. friendly, professional, celebratory)
- Indicate Hebrew as the language

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Telegram Trigger".
Open the "Ultimate Assistant" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Contact Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Content Creator Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Tavily" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Calendar Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download File" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Transcribe" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Calculator1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch1" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Response1" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wikipedia" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Qdrant Vector Store" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Default Data Loader" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Recursive Character Text Splitter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Qdrant Vector Store1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download File1" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set 'Text'1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Edit Fields".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Edit Fields1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
